title: Indulgent Umami Greek Lamb Kleftiko
description: Rich and savory, this traditional Greek dish is slow-cooked to perfection with tender lamb and a depth of umami flavor. Serve with crusty bread or over rice.

ingredients.name: leg of lamb
ingredients.notes: preferably shio-cure, or other cured lamb

instructions: In a large bowl, combine lamb, salt, black pepper, and garlic cloves. Mix well to coat the lamb evenly. Let it sit for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until browned, about 5 minutes per side. Remove the lamb from the pot and set it aside.
Reduce heat to medium and saut3 onion in the pot until it's softened and lightly caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Add lemon juice, red wine, and honey to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it's slightly reduced, about 5 minutes.
Return the lamb to the pot, cover it with a lid, and transfer it to the oven. Braise the lamb at 3009F for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until it's tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the lamb from the pot and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
Strain the braising liquid and discard the solids. Skim off any excess fat that rises to the surface.
Serve the lamb with the reduced braising liquid spooned over the top, garnished with fresh parsley and dill
